data.narrative:
World Labs, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall, is valued at $5.0B+ post-money on $1.23B+ raised. The relevant comparables universe comprises 1 public peer (Palantir) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, including Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, Nominal, and Photoroom.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $5.0B last post-money valuation with a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies $9.5B. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $3.3B, with a range from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). The wide peer dispersion reflects substantial variance in maturity and capital intensity within early-stage AI companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $10.9B, with a median of $6.4B. The current $5.0B+ valuation sits in the lower-middle portion of this range. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier in a volatile frontier-tech market and the relevance of outlier comparables (Safe Superintelligence at $32B) to World Labs' specific business model and trajectory. Peer-median analysis alone suggests potential upside, though stage-progression implies modest further appreciation.
